NOTES ON ILLUSTRATIONS

PAGE
James Madison ........... Frontispiece

Sir Henry Clinton.--Reproduced from an old steel engraving
after a painting by Smart ............ 3

Rodney Triumphant. From Gillray's Caricature
Upon The Occasion Of Sir George Rodney's victory
over Admiral de Grasse in the West Indies.--Reproduced
from the print in the New York Public Library
(Lenox Building). This signal victory of Rodney over de
Grasse was considered the most important battle of the century,
and as a reward for his services Rodney was raised
to the peerage. The print shows Rodney trampling on the
French flag while receiving de Grasse's sword. In the background
are sailors bringing ashore the treasure-chests, while
above Rodney is seen the Viscount's coronet, which never
reached his head ................ 5

Yale College and Chapel, 1786.--Reproduced from an old
print in the library at Yale University, New Haven, Conn. 7

Henry Seymour Conway.--Reproduced from a print in the
European Magazine, 1782 ............. 9

North side of Wall Street, New York, 1785.--Redrawn
from an old print ................ 10

Lord Shelburne.--Reproduced from an old steel engraving 11

South Carolina Currency.--From the original in the New
York Public Library (Lenox Building) ........ 13

General Carleton's Return Of Refugees.--From The
Memorial History of New York City ......... 14
